name: Sarkaz's Furnaceside Fables
line:
The voices of the revenants draw you away from reality.
A ritual, a communion.
An otherworldly experience is about to begin.

zones.name: Furnace Origin
zones.desc: Verse one. Familiar warmth fades into the darkness as reality erodes at every turn.

zones.name: Wrought Ironroots
zones.desc: Line two. The winding past connects to an unprecedented future as the spores of imagination grow within every pipe.

zones.name: Ashforged Ruins
zones.desc: Paragraph three. Do you recognize this city//wreck//palace//village?

zones.name: Probable Divergence
zones.desc: Section four. At imagination's peak, Kazdel is both bountiful and hollow. It craves, it hungers, always wanting more, oblivious to the encroaching boundaries.

zones.name: Border of Truth
zones.desc: Chapter five. The end, unity, and stability—all torn asunder. Subverting common sense is the only path to a new chapter.

zones.name: Radiant Zenith
zones.desc: Article six. Rebirth ushers in an immeasurable imagination, both unexpected and reasonable. Accept the curse//blessing.

zones.name: Temple of Shattered Shackles
zones.desc: Article six. The Dharma has been transmitted, and to hold it is to reach ultimate freedom. But can thusness be followed, be sought? If only with great difficulty, can it be held on to?

zones.name: Endless Rest
zones.desc: Coda. Every story stops here. The light gradually dims, and the furnace sounds cease.

nodeTypes.name: Combat Ops
nodeTypes.desc: Everyone has their weapons drawn. They're definitely not here to negotiate.
nodeTypes.func: A standard combat node. Win to earn collectibles, Originium Ingots, and other rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Emergency Ops
nodeTypes.desc: Some violence may well exceed your expectations, a very common occurrence.
nodeTypes.func: A harder fight with stronger enemies than regular Combat Ops — and better rewards (Hope, etc.) to match.

nodeTypes.name: Dreadful Foe
nodeTypes.desc: Criminals, ruffians, and the damned… You're in trouble.
nodeTypes.func: The boss battle guarding the end of each floor. You must clear it to move on to the next floor.

nodeTypes.name: Safe House
nodeTypes.desc: Nobody will bother you here, and there's plenty of food and water. You can finally relax.
nodeTypes.func: A rest node offering upkeep options such as restoring Objective HP or temporarily promoting Operators.

nodeTypes.name: Encounter
nodeTypes.desc: New friends, old rivals… Strange encounters never age. Only scars stand the test of time.
nodeTypes.func: A random-event node. Depending on your choices you gain rewards — or pay a price.

nodeTypes.name: Downtime Recreation
nodeTypes.desc: Indulging in life's simpler pleasures helps combat the monotony and hardships of your long journey ahead.
nodeTypes.func: A recreation node with theme-specific minigames and wagers. Rewards depend on the outcome.

nodeTypes.name: Dense Fog
nodeTypes.desc: There's something in the mist, but you cannot see anything...
nodeTypes.func: An unidentified node hidden from view. Its true type is revealed when you enter.

nodeTypes.name: Wish Fulfilled
nodeTypes.desc: There are many ways of making a wish, but the wish itself is almost always the same.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you pick one recruitment voucher from a random selection.

nodeTypes.name: Lost and Found
nodeTypes.desc: The void of helplessness will be filled by the joy and satisfaction of gain.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you trade what you have (Objective HP, collectibles, etc.) for other rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Scout
nodeTypes.desc: Someone will always have to take the first step, to show the way for those who come after.
nodeTypes.func: A node that sends some of your Operators on a detached mission. They leave your squad for a while and bring back rewards on return.

nodeTypes.name: Rogue Trader
nodeTypes.desc: Neither an ally nor an enemy. Some people like to sit on the fence.
nodeTypes.func: A shop where you spend Originium Ingots on collectibles and items, or restore Objective HP.

nodeTypes.name: Possibility
nodeTypes.desc: Throw your dreams into the frame, and transform your fragmented ideas into brand new plans.
nodeTypes.func: An entrance to a special area. Entering leads to a separate zone or event.

nodeTypes.name: False to Truth
nodeTypes.desc: The changes are so remarkably real that you think you can tell true from false.
nodeTypes.func: A refining node that converts Thoughts into other products. A Thought's rarity affects the quality of the result.

nodeTypes.name: Face-Off
nodeTypes.desc: This has happened to the Sarkaz too many times to count.
nodeTypes.func: A special battle where you choose your opponent. Losing costs no Objective HP, and harder opponents give better rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Prophecy
nodeTypes.desc: The choice lies before us, the outcome already set.
nodeTypes.func: A story-event node tied to ending branches. It appears at specific spots depending on your progress and holdings, and can lead to hidden ending routes.

nodeTypes.name: Prophecy
nodeTypes.desc: The choice lies before us, the outcome already set.
nodeTypes.func: A story-event node tied to ending branches. It appears at specific spots depending on your progress and holdings, and can lead to hidden ending routes.

difficulties.rule: Monthly squads are refreshed on the first day of each month

difficulties.name: Deep Dive Mode
difficulties.rule: A challenge mode that provides a unique experience.

stages.name: Bad Neighbors
stages.desc: A group of tramps and their pets have taken over this maintenance channel, modifying it without regard for others.

stages.name: Vermin
stages.desc:
The furnace's heat attracts all kinds of critters from the wastes. They eagerly slip inside and gleefully wreak havoc.
 that deal Arts damage for brief periods when activated are present on the field

stages.name: Safety Inspection
stages.desc:
The Liches have declared that maintenance workers must be examined and counted when they leave the furnace—not one less, and certainly not one more. This rule is not always enforced.
 that greatly increases the ATK and ASPD of units that pass over it, but continuously reduces their HP is present on the field
 that deploy enemy mercenary drones are present on the field.

stages.name: Hasty Escape
stages.desc: These embezzling Sarkaz sought to steal treasure from the furnace, but the 'treasure' discovered them first. In this chase, any road is a way out to survival.

stages.name: Ice Age
stages.desc:
Making use of your environment is a fundamental survival skill, and the ice traps have already been set. The rest is up to you.
 that continuously apply [Cold] are present on the field.

stages.name: Dissuasion Seminar
stages.desc: Regularly hosted by the Temple of Knowledge, the Liches inside the furnace recount vivid horrors they've faced, urging others to leave. No one ever does.

stages.name: Temporary Patchup
stages.desc:
Furnace maintenance tip #1: If something's busted, tear it down—if it needs repairing, someone will come and do it.
 that can be activated with DP to stun surrounding enemies is present on the field.

stages.name: Exhaust Vents
stages.desc:
Most Sarkaz youths have boasted of jumping off the platforms by the furnace exhaust vents and gliding down with the wind. In reality, those 'platforms' don't even have solid footing.
 that generate airflow currents are present on the field

stages.name: Furnace Volunteers
stages.desc:
They were getting off work but were in such a hurry that they ended up in the wrong Kazdel.
 that deal damage to both allies and enemies are present on the field

stages.name: Clear the Crowd
stages.desc:
After figuring out what happened, these Sarkaz begin arguing over which Kazdel is better. They invite you to arbitrate the dispute, and won't leave until they're satisfied with the result.
 are present on the field; Operators deployed on this tile will not be targeted by enemy ranged attacks

stages.name: Kaz Falls
stages.desc:
This place is famed for its unique living waterfall and bizarre life cycle. When enough slugkaz fall into the valley, they transform into machines, swim upstream, and revert to organic nourishment.
Obtain extra Originium Ingots and Command EXP upon victory

stages.name: Dense Jungle
stages.desc: Though they're called Slug Eaters, they'll devour anything that fits in their mouths.

stages.name: Simulated Skirmish
stages.desc:
No one wants to play the role of 'foreign foe' in drills like this, so these mock skirmishes always turn into Sarkaz versus Sarkaz.
 that halves the DEF of allied units deployed on it is present on the field

stages.name: Bloodline Debates
stages.desc: A classic debate topic in the Temple of Knowledge: How exactly did the Teekaz split off, and when in history did it happen? Scholars endlessly speculate, with new theories 'invented' from time to time.

stages.name: Blotted Sky
stages.desc: Kazdel's lifts always have long queues, with Nachzehrers handling uninvited guests trying to sneak into the city. Their summons also provide shade for everyone waiting.

stages.name: Morning Labor
stages.desc:
In the city, crystal clusters grow nonstop, making morning harvesting a daily habit for the Sarkaz. Those who can afford breakfast harvest after eating; those who can't, go straight away.
 that can change the enemy's route is present on the field

stages.name: Careless Magic
stages.desc:
When cooking, accidents are prone to happen if the fire is neglected. The same is true when summoning servants with witchcraft. These two common sense rules are sometimes forgotten by the Sarkaz.
 that deal damage to both allies and enemies are present on the field

stages.name: Lottery Fight
stages.desc:
Invented by well-traveled Sarkaz for the thrill of it, the 'prizes' in this lottery step into the ring. It always ends in a chaotic group scrap, but at least everyone's having fun.
 are present on the field that Block enemies
 that continuously apply [Cold] are present on the field

stages.name: Firepower Squad
stages.desc:
Scarmarket Nexus's firepower squads are armed with excessively many bomb drones and rocket launchers to carpet bomb enemies at first sight. This strategy reminds you of a certain other Sarkaz you know well.
Obtain extra Originium Ingots and Command EXP upon victory

stages.name: Motorized Squad
stages.desc: This Sarkaz squad is regularly deployed to the ground to collect enemy intelligence for the Sky City.

stages.name: Epochal Gaps
stages.desc:
Time releases its grip, and the Sarkaz memories flicker through the intervals of existence.
 that grant a certain amount of DP and Max DP when destroyed are present on the field

stages.name: Frequent Dispute
stages.desc:
The Tarans' arrival has only complicated the mine's ownership dispute. Ultimately, the solution is violence.
 pieces that can block the enemy's route are present on the map.

stages.name: Sordid Surveillance
stages.desc: Illusions ebb and flow under witchcraft, with balance meticulously monitored across all epochs to ensure events unfold in their designated order.

stages.name: Past The Aquapit
stages.desc:
A Durin from who-knows-where learned the skill of low-altitude flight from the Scrap Assemblers and now relishes in flight contests through the gaps between buildings in Kazdel. After all, the worst you'll get is a head full of bumps.
 that block enemies are present on the field

stages.name: Crimson Corridor
stages.desc:
This corridor will bring you to the Vampire's mausoleum, but it has yet to be stained with blood. This is precisely why Vampire heirs are scrambling to dye it in their shade right now.
 that periodically inflict damage to units standing on them are present on the field
 that block enemies are present on the field

stages.name: Rules of Modern Warfare
stages.desc:
Several months have passed since the Sarkaz Joint Agreement was enacted. All involved parties stand ready, as this new blood pact prepares to face its first trial in the flames of war.
 that activate by absorbing experimental products are present on the field

stages.desc: Some ancient Sarkaz have been angered by these machines they've never seen before, but their outer shells seem to sport an engraved symbol of Kazdel.

stages.name: Ghost City
stages.desc:
Legends say a revenant has possessed an ancient nomadic plate and wanders the land to this day. Could it be this very plate before you? What kind of people live on it?
 that produce Rot Eaters are present on the field

She has many names and many identities in different tales, but if you were to ask what she's like, the answer is always 'loves explosions'.

A mercenary went to Londinium to make ends meet, but you firmly believe she has nothing to do with the great blaze that destroyed the city.

stages.desc: The Sarkaz reclaim land with brutal efficiency: bombard the area until it's flat. With mouths to feed, careless interlopers won't deter their relentless plans.

stages.desc:
Led by the Emperor and nobles, the Leithanians gradually grew accustomed to life with the Sarkaz. Little do they know, this genuine friendship will soon bring shocking secrets.

stages.desc: This channel must be sealed off, and any negligence eradicated within the furnace. Illusions hold truth and can shape reality, so act with extreme precaution.

stages.name: Guiltless Paradise
stages.desc:
Chaos is order, creation is law.

stages.name: Aerial Assault
stages.desc: Kazdel's 'plate' sails through the skies, but regrettably, this does not mean it is impervious to assaults from hostile cities.

stages.name: Wrecked Academy
stages.desc:
One night, parts of the academy tumbled into Kazdel's many cracks. The next day, unaffected scholars and professors were anguished, lamenting their missed opportunity.

The two greatest wishes of the Teekaz outside the Holy City are acceptance and entry. The Gelato Stops within the city will take care of the rest.

stages.name: Heavenly Paradise
stages.desc: 'They say paradise should be offered four treasures: a mirror with wings of copper, a wooden paperweight of gathered strength, a seal of passage engraved with an auspicious beast, and a flawless spearhead of iron.'

stages.name: 'Heretics'
stages.desc: Perhaps labeling this rabble 'heretics' is too good for them. They're not even followers of another path. They simply want to tear down your flag for their own satisfaction.

stages.name: Signal Light
stages.desc:
Who knows what unhinged plans the Duck Lord had in mind when he arranged a vehicle to abduct civilians. Fortunately, you found out about it in advance, and the drivers don't know the route too well.

enemy_1224_dsuply.ability: While in battle, Reborn Creations have increased ATK, and their attacks will additionally deal Necrosis

enemy_1220_dzoms.name: Touch of the Sanguinarch
enemy_1220_dzoms.desc: A creation of the Sanguinarch of Vampires' Originium Arts, flexible and difficult to defeat. According to rumors, the Sanguinarch is able to snatch the blood of other creatures, imbuing new life into it and taking it under his control.
enemy_1220_dzoms.ability:
Reborn Creation (takes greatly reduced Physical and Arts damage)
Units blocking it gain +1 Block (up to a maximum of +6)

enemy_1082_soticn.name: Guerrilla Mortar
enemy_1082_soticn.desc: One of Patriot's elite suppression troops. Adept at long-ranged shelling, these fighters move more slowly because of their medium armor but also enjoy increased protection. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain greatly increased combat capabilities and have significantly increased attack speed.
enemy_1082_soticn.ability: When Inspired, gains greatly increased ASPD.

enemy_1223_dmech.name: Sarkaz Heirbearer Artificer
enemy_1223_dmech.desc: An artificer of the Sarkaz Royal Court blessed with the living blood of the Sanguinarch of Vampires. They gained a portion of Victoria's engineering expertise and are responsible for operating the reloading system of Londinium's city defense artillery.
enemy_1223_dmech.ability:
Continuously charges up Londinium Secondary Defense Artillery; when interrupted, the Defense Artillery will lose a portion of its charge progress
Will be reborn as a  after falling in battle for the first time

enemy_1222_dpvt.name: Sarkaz Heirbearer Warrior
enemy_1222_dpvt.desc: A warrior of the Sarkaz Royal Court blessed with the living blood of the Sanguinarch of Vampires. After falling in battle for the first time, his blood will continue to advance in his place.
enemy_1222_dpvt.ability: Will be reborn as a  after falling in battle for the first time

enemy_1228_dslime.name: Bloodthirsty Heir
enemy_1228_dslime.desc: A creation of the Sanguinarch of Vampires' Originium Arts. They always thirst for more blood, strengthening themselves by stealing the blood of others.
enemy_1228_dslime.ability: Attacks restore HP proportional to damage dealt

enemy_1021_bslime.name: Infused Originium Slug
enemy_1021_bslime.desc: An infected wild creature that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen, exploding upon death. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.
enemy_1021_bslime.ability: Death deals a large amount of Physical damage to surrounding units

enemy_1271_nhkodo.name: Sarkaz Wither Devourer
enemy_1271_nhkodo.desc: Part of the Nachzehrer army. Devours any obstacle in its way, including the living. Anything and everything is food to it.
enemy_1271_nhkodo.ability:
When blocked for the first time, devours the blocker. DEF is greatly increased, but Movement Speed is reduced
Returns devoured Operator when defeated

enemy_1269_nhfly.name: Seed of Withering
enemy_1269_nhfly.desc: Originium Arts creation of the Nachzehrer King. Mindless except for an obsession with self-destruction. They create death for others to feed upon.
enemy_1269_nhfly.ability:
Flying unit.
Self-destructs when near an Operator, dealing Physical damage

enemy_1415_mmkabi.name: Armored Burdenbeast
enemy_1415_mmkabi.desc: Large, slow, and docile, these burdenbeasts obediently undergo rigorous training to perform tasks that go against their nature.
enemy_1415_mmkabi.ability: Will be tripped by a Stabilizer Chain, stunning itself and dealing Physical damage to nearby enemies.

enemy_1009_lurker.name: Lurker
enemy_1009_lurker.desc: One of Reunion's special soldiers. They move swiftly and stealthily, making it almost impossible to detect them before they engage in combat. Therefore, teamwork is needed in order to defeat them.

enemy_1221_dzomg.name: Gift of the Sanguinarch
enemy_1221_dzomg.desc: A creation of the Sanguinarch of Vampires' Originium Arts that bears greater vitality. One who was powerful in life will continue to be a great threat to their enemies even after being reshaped by the power of the Sanguinarch's blessing.
enemy_1221_dzomg.ability:
Reborn Creation (takes greatly reduced Physical and Arts damage)
Units blocking it gain +1 Block (up to a maximum of +6)

enemy_1085_sotiwz.name: Sarkaz Guerrilla Caster
enemy_1085_sotiwz.desc: A Sarkaz Caster who supports Patriot but cannot understand the special terminology of their guerilla operations. The Sarkaz ritual maintained by the Sarkaz Originium Altars will not damage them, instead strengthening their bodies and converting their attacks into Arts damage.
enemy_1085_sotiwz.ability:
Casts Arts periodically, dealing Arts damage equivalent to unit's ATK to all surrounding units
When coming into contact with a Pulse Wave, this unit instead takes no damage and their attack range expands

enemy_1322_wdgyht.name: 'Trilby Asher'
enemy_1322_wdgyht.desc: 'Forever honor one's commitments,' the proud motto of House Caster. While the Duke's military and political might are more than enough to back their word, the 'Trilby Ashers' are the real reason why the Duke of Caster's words hold any weight.
enemy_1322_wdgyht.ability: After several attacks, moves towards the next checkpoint for the next attack

enemy_1384_winfrz.name: Frozen Mountain Burdenbeast
enemy_1384_winfrz.desc: Mountain burdenbeast buried by a sudden avalanche. Its thick layer of fat will help it survive the worst of it until rescue arrives.
enemy_1384_winfrz.ability:
Remains on standby in  areas for a while without attacking.
Immediately revived if its plate is in Emergency Heater range

enemy_1189_krgaxe.name: Icefield Warrior
enemy_1189_krgaxe.desc: A warrior proficient in icefield combat. Long periods of living on the icefields have tempered their will and physique.

enemy_1192_krgscr_2.name: Icefield Caster Leader
enemy_1192_krgscr_2.desc: A caster who has secluded themselves in the mountains to research Arts techniques. The frost has become a part of their Arts, or rather their Arts have become a part of the icefields.
enemy_1192_krgscr_2.ability:
Attacks 2 enemies simultaneously
Every 3rd attack inflicts Cold to the target
Gains status resistance

enemy_1388_wingnt.name: 'Catapult'
enemy_1388_wingnt.desc: Expert grenadiers of the delegation, able to throw shells as heavy as a hundred kilos. The rest of the delegation knows better than to challenge them to a snowball fight.
enemy_1388_wingnt.ability:
Throws a massive snowball, dealing splash damage in a cross
Can only use melee attacks once snowball is thrown

enemy_1040_bombd.name: Bombtail
enemy_1040_bombd.desc: An unmanned enemy drone that flies slowly. They carry an explosive warhead that is launched when approaching nearby Operators, inflicting physical damage in an area. Their design seems to have been inspired by a fantasy creature. Though they cannot attack anymore after launching the warhead, their movement speed increases afterwards due to the loss of weight.
enemy_1040_bombd.ability:
Flying unit.
Can launch a warhead to deal AoE physical damage
Gains significantly increased Movement Speed after launching warhead

enemy_1144_merlgd.name: War Phantom
enemy_1144_merlgd.desc: The 'War Phantoms' were once a legendary band of mercenaries. In the wake of their bloody legacy, mercenaries and bounty hunters began referring to powerful, cruel veteran warriors with the same title. Strong and adept at controlling multiple types of Air Support Drones.
enemy_1144_merlgd.ability:
On first attack, immediately dispatches  to support the battle from the Mobile Tactical Hangar
The first time HP drops below half, immediately dispatches  from the Mobile Tactical Hangar to support the battle
When on the field, all Air Support Drones gain greatly increased Movement Speed

enemy_1143_merrpg.name: Anti-Armor Infantry
enemy_1143_merrpg.desc: A mercenary specializing in anti-armor combat. Carries a single-use grenade launcher which is discarded after firing along with other non-essential equipment, after which they charge at high speed towards the enemy.
enemy_1143_merrpg.ability:
Can fire a super long-range grenade, dealing AoE Physical damage
After firing, significantly increases Movement Speed and can only perform melee attacks

enemy_1146_defspd.name: Anvil
enemy_1146_defspd.desc: The enemy's defensive Air Support Drones. It strengthens the DEF of enemy units within range and slowly recovers their HP.
enemy_1146_defspd.ability:
Flying unit.
Continually provides boosted DEF and HP recovery to nearby enemy units
Difficult to attack
Will automatically retreat some time after launch

enemy_1171_durokt.name: Dublinn Flying Soldier
enemy_1171_durokt.desc: A flying soldier in the Dublinn forces. The steam propulsion device they use originates from a secret laboratory owned by the Victorian military. As it has only recently been deployed in battle, its power output is still unstable.

enemy_1418_mmkonm.name: 'Tinkerer'
enemy_1418_mmkonm.desc: Sarkaz artificers obsessed with building a flying machine. The patches on the balloon mend not just the vehicle, but the heart of a dreamer as well.
enemy_1418_mmkonm.ability:
Low-Altitude Hovering
Attack range reduced when it loses Low-Altitude Hovering.

enemy_1082_soticn_2.name: Guerrilla Mortar Leader
enemy_1082_soticn_2.desc: The cream of the crop among Patriot's elite suppression troops. Compared to ordinary mortars, these troops equip more dangerous and destructive shells. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain greatly increased combat capabilities and have significantly increased attack speed.
enemy_1082_soticn_2.ability: When Inspired, gains greatly increased ASPD.

enemy_1313_wdfmr.name: Norport Civilian
enemy_1313_wdfmr.desc: Ordinary folk scattered throughout Norport Borough. Abandoned by Londinium and subjected to the Sarkaz's high-handed blockade, these orphans have become the ones most deeply affected by the haze of war.
enemy_1313_wdfmr.ability:
Friendly Unit
Once their identity is confirmed, restores your max DP after retreating to the Objective Point

enemy_1316_wdpjd.name: Armed Spy
enemy_1316_wdpjd.desc: A spy hired by a mysterious employer to sabotage their rivals while disguised as civilians. They are more or less professional soldiers with training in gathering intelligence, and their reliable weaponry and ample combat experience make them even more of a threat.

enemy_1317_wdexg_2.name: Sarkaz Absurdist Operative Leader
enemy_1317_wdexg_2.desc: Operatives attached to the Military Commission, primarily tasked with assassinating high-value targets. When on a mission, they often arrive before their subordinates to soften the enemy frontlines with incredible speed, while simultaneously planning their next action.
enemy_1317_wdexg_2.ability: Attacks reduce DP when carrying ammo. Ammo capacity: 3

enemy_1320_wdrrl_2.name: Sarkaz Absurdist Tyrant Captain
enemy_1320_wdrrl_2.desc: A product of the cooperation between the Damazti Cluster and the Military Commission, able to change their appearance with Arts. They can skillfully manipulate their flesh without damaging their brains, and are used to employing their intimidating forms to gain an advantage in battle.
enemy_1320_wdrrl_2.ability:
Can only be blocked by Operators with 3 or more Block
First attack deals Splash damage

enemy_2066_skzlcp.name: Sarkaz Maze Spellweaver
enemy_2066_skzlcp.desc: A vigilant Lich, one of the many custodians of the Temple of Knowledge. His threads entwine with the sparks in the Soul Furnace, rescuing unwitting wanderers and punishing trespassers.
enemy_2066_skzlcp.ability: Stops attacking and starts channeling to create Marks of Epoch around the Objective.

enemy_1229_darmy.name: Sarkaz Royal Court Soldier
enemy_1229_darmy.desc: A soldier of the Sarkaz Royal Court. Londinium's munitions factories work day and night to produce weapons for the Sarkaz war effort. They boast a distinctly Sarkaz fighting style, but with greater discipline.

enemy_1045_hammer.name: Defense Crusher
enemy_1045_hammer.desc: One of Reunion's senior elites, clad in a heavy blast suit. Each swing from their weapon is slow but packs tremendous destructive power. Operators will be stunned after taking several attacks.Proceed with caution.
enemy_1045_hammer.ability: After two attacks, the next attack Stuns its target

enemy_1412_mmjump.name: Scrap Assembler
enemy_1412_mmjump.desc: Mercenaries fascinated with building new equipment and moving heavier equipment by adding more power.
enemy_1412_mmjump.ability: Temporarily takes flight when blocked or facing a Stabilizer Chain for the first time. DEF is greatly reduced after landing.

enemy_1226_dklord.name: Sarkaz Heirbearer Hatedrinker
enemy_1226_dklord.desc: A soldier of the Sarkaz Royal Court blessed by the Sanguinarch of Vampires. The hatred of their compatriots slain on the battlefield builds up within them, making them more than eager to tear apart the enemies of the Sarkaz.
enemy_1226_dklord.ability:
Gains increased ATK for each Reborn Creation on the battlefield
Will be reborn as a  after falling in battle for the first time

enemy_1249_lysdb.name: Rhine Defense Member
enemy_1249_lysdb.desc: Guard personnel of Rhine Lab. With their company-provided anti-riot gear and training, they are qualified for escort duties.
enemy_1249_lysdb.ability: Blocks 1 instance of Physical or Arts damage

enemy_1250_lyengs.name: Rhine Engineering Member
enemy_1250_lyengs.desc: An engineer of Rhine Lab. Responsible for both providing equipment and giving their professional opinion on its production, they also maintain a close relationship with the Energy Section.

enemy_1251_lysyta.name: Dor-1 Failure
enemy_1251_lysyta.desc: A product of Dorothy's failed experiments. An irregularly-shaped silver object emanating a soft glow, like a slumbering Dreamland.
enemy_1251_lysyta.ability: When touching an inactive , the Power Armor recovers some SP

enemy_1255_lybgpa_2.name: R-31a Heavy Power Armor
enemy_1255_lybgpa_2.desc: A model that appeared around half a year ago among some mercenary groups, its performance is on par with mass-produced military models from five years ago. Technology is unregulated, so Columbians continue to seek greater efficiency within the bounds of the law.
enemy_1255_lybgpa_2.ability: [Overload] Continuously deals Physical damage to the target proportional to the unit's ATK, andStuns the target

enemy_1088_ltsmer.name: Leithanian Whisperer
enemy_1088_ltsmer.desc: An Arts user commonly seen throughout Leithanien. They attack using Arts, and will cast on L-44 'Gramophones' within their attack range to attempt to seize control of them.
enemy_1088_ltsmer.ability: Continuously casts at the nearest non-enemy controlled 'Gramophone' within attack range to attempt to seize control of it.

enemy_1256_lyacpa.name: R-11 Assault Power Armor
enemy_1256_lyacpa.desc: A model equipped with long-ranged weapons, massively increasing its offensive capabilities. While these experimental weapons are still in the prototype phase, its energy and firepower cannot be overlooked.

enemy_1074_dbskar_2.name: Sarkaz Worldcurser
enemy_1074_dbskar_2.desc: An elite Sarkaz caster mercenary. Their Originium-wracked bodies have become convenient tools in their operations, and their accumulated resentment has crystallized into raw power. Though they won't attack while on Standby, they will cruelly expend their own life to complete their mission once in Combat-Ready mode.
enemy_1074_dbskar_2.ability: Starts battle in Standby and will not attack; upon being injured, becomes Combat-Ready and attacks two targets with long-ranged Arts damage.

enemy_1336_bldbat.name: Vesperwing
enemy_1336_bldbat.desc: An Infected Creature that appears in dark places, often found in deep caves and abandoned buildings. It is easily startled, and will not hesitate to attack intruders who enter its territory.

enemy_1266_nhapos.name: Rot Eater
enemy_1266_nhapos.desc: Part of the Nachzehrer army. The longer they remain on the battlefield, the harder they are to defeat, absorbing the enemy's strength as they grow increasingly deadly.
enemy_1266_nhapos.ability:
The more enemies defeated, the higher its HP, ATK, and DEF when spawned from the Twigs That Crave
Releases Corruption, causing your nearby units to continuously lose HP

enemy_1504_cqbw.name: W
enemy_1504_cqbw.desc: A Sarkaz Mercenary and one of Reunion's squad leaders, she carries around a weapon that resembles a Laterano Gun. However, she almost exclusively attacks using explosives and throwing weapons. One of her common strategies is to attack the enemy's flank using large amounts of active Originium explosives. However, her erratic movements make her almost impossible to predict...
enemy_1504_cqbw.ability:
Can use explosives to deal heavy Physical damage to allied units
Uses more explosives when HP is under 50%

enemy_1288_duskls.name: Dublinn Flamechaser Soldier
enemy_1288_duskls.desc: A Dublinn soldier fused with their Leader's ember. Long since dead, but Arts manipulation keeps their bodies moving towards their final desire, rising again and again.
enemy_1288_duskls.ability:
Attacks deal additional Burn while near burning Reeds

enemy_1042_frostd.name: Frost
enemy_1042_frostd.desc: An unmanned enemy drone equipped with heavy armor to make it harder to destroy. Its prototype was designed by a graphics engine studio. They are equipped with a special device that allows them to drastically reduce the temperature around them, greatly decreasing the attack speed of nearby Operators.
enemy_1042_frostd.ability:
Flying unit.
All friendly units within range have reduced ASPD

enemy_1090_mdsnpr_2.name: Mudrock Matterllurgist Leader
enemy_1090_mdsnpr_2.desc: A member of Mudrock's Sarkaz squad. Integrating Sarkaz tactics with Leithanian Arts to greater effect, they are capable of inflicting lethal damage with a block of energy, and prioritize attacking units closest to the contract entrance site.
enemy_1090_mdsnpr_2.ability:
Prioritizes attacking units that are vying for control of a 'Gramophone.'
Attacks 2 targets simultaneously

enemy_1270_nhstlk.name: Rotchaser
enemy_1270_nhstlk.desc: Originium Arts creation of the Nachzehrer King. Fast-moving, they do not understand the concept of healing. They exist only to create death, and the power of healing robs them of their powers.
enemy_1270_nhstlk.ability: Attacks cause the target to take Arts damage over time; effect removed when the target is healed

enemy_1175_dushdo_2.name: Dublinn Elite Companion Shadowblade
enemy_1175_dushdo_2.desc: A member of the Dublinn forces' elite operations squad, they are much stronger than regular squad members. When they move alongside their partners, they gain powerful stealth capabilities and their attacks are much swifter.
enemy_1175_dushdo_2.ability:
Invisible

enemy_1174_duholy_2.name: Dublinn Elite Companion Guard
enemy_1174_duholy_2.desc: A member of the Dublinn forces' elite operations squad, they are much stronger than regular squad members. The shields they wield are the culmination of many nations' technologies, providing cover for their partners while advancing, and protecting all nearby allies from harm.
enemy_1174_duholy_2.ability:
More likely to get attacked by your units

enemy_1171_durokt_2.name: Dublinn Flying Squad Leader
enemy_1171_durokt_2.desc: A flying squad leader in the Dublinn forces. The steam propulsion device they use originates from a secret laboratory owned by the Victorian military and has gone through many iterations. Its power output is relatively stable.

enemy_1076_bsthmr_2.name: Demolitionist Leader
enemy_1076_bsthmr_2.desc: A high-level elite Reunion combatant who wears heavy-duty, explosion-proof protective gear, more aggressive than normal Demolitionists. Their special Originium-powered hammers have massive destructive range, and must be dealt with cautiously.
enemy_1076_bsthmr_2.ability: Attacks cause an explosion, dealing Splash damage to units around the primary target.

enemy_1511_mdrock.name: Mudrock
enemy_1511_mdrock.desc: Age unknown, gender unknown, history unknown. This ex-Reunion member now leads an organization of Infected and wanders the wilderness between the cities of Leithanien. What thoughts run through Mudrock's mind as being forced to once again step upon the battlefield?
enemy_1511_mdrock.ability:
Every attack permanently increases ATK, stacking up to 6 times
Has a Barrier that absorbs Arts damage; while the Barrier persists, this unit has significantly increased Max HP and ASPD.
Barrier periodically refreshes.
Can use a skill to automatically seize control of the nearest non-enemy controlled 'Gramophone'

enemy_1275_dwlock.name: Sarkaz Royal Court Caster
enemy_1275_dwlock.desc: A caster of the Sarkaz Royal Court. Sarkaz witchcraft is often more dangerous in battle than war machines. They boast a distinctly Sarkaz fighting style, but with greater discipline.
enemy_1275_dwlock.ability:
Attacks deal additional Necrosis
Locks on to your units, dealing continuous Arts damage, and causing area Necrosis when casting is complete

enemy_1273_stmgun.name: Londinium Mobile Defense Artillery
enemy_1273_stmgun.desc: Part of Londinium's city defenses. Has a large attack area. Built by the city's workers in their factories.
enemy_1273_stmgun.ability:
Capable of long-range attacks

enemy_1274_stmram.name: Steam Tank
enemy_1274_stmram.desc: Armored vehicle employing Victorian steam technology, even more dangerous when it has taken damage.
enemy_1274_stmram.ability: When HP drops to 50%, ASPD and Movement Speed are greatly increased, and becomes immune to most control effects.

enemy_1267_nhpbr_2.name: Sarkaz Witherer Captain
enemy_1267_nhpbr_2.desc: Elite member of the Nachzehrer army. Death simply causes them to spread their corruption even faster, leaving behind a wasteland where few could survive.
enemy_1267_nhpbr_2.ability: Releases Corruption when defeated, causing your nearby units to continuously lose HP

enemy_1371_ltnflu.name: Remnant Orchestra Flautist
enemy_1371_ltnflu.desc: Flautist who wants to join their king's orchestra. This nobody has never seen the spectacle, and is all the more fanatical for it.
enemy_1371_ltnflu.ability: After 2 attacks, the next attack has increased ATK

enemy_1375_ltnclr.name: Remnant Orchestra Wind Player
enemy_1375_ltnclr.desc: Wind player who wants to become the concertmaster of their king's orchestra. He worked hard to climb the ladder, but the orchestra is long gone, for which he is very resentful.
enemy_1375_ltnclr.ability:
ATK continuously increases when unblocked. ATK is reset after the next attack.
When melancholic variation is active, ATK is greatly increased, and attacks Stun the target

enemy_1377_ltnclo.name: Remnant Orchestra Cellist
enemy_1377_ltnclo.desc: Cellist who wants to be their king's trusted henchman. He lives to earn their king's favor, for which he is willing to play obsequious tunes.
enemy_1377_ltnclo.ability:
More likely to be attacked by your units
When melancholic variation is active, ATK is greatly increased, and gains a Shield that protects against a certain number of attacks; each time damage is prevented, reflects as Physical damage based on own ATK

enemy_1271_nhkodo_2.name: Sarkaz Wither Maw
enemy_1271_nhkodo_2.desc: Elite member of the Nachzehrer army. Devours any obstacle in its way, including the living. Anything and everything is food to it. Only death can make it surrender what it devoured.
enemy_1271_nhkodo_2.ability:
When blocked for the first time, devours the blocker. DEF is greatly increased, but Movement Speed is reduced
Returns devoured Operator when defeated

enemy_1513_dekght.name: Corrupted Knight
enemy_1513_dekght.desc: A Sarkaz knight with a broken horn who radiates terror and shows clear signs of excessive drug use. Uses a warhammer in battle. Crushes enemies with terrifying bloodlust. When the Withered Knight falls, takes up the quest and becomes dramatically stronger. Every step he takes spreads corruption.
enemy_1513_dekght.ability:
Attacks deal Physical damage to the target and adjacent allied units
Does charged attacks, dealing heavy Physical damage to the targets and any adjacent units
Gains greatly increased ATK, ASPD, and Movement Speed when  dies

enemy_1513_dekght_2.name: Withered Knight
enemy_1513_dekght_2.desc: A Sarkaz knight with a broken horn who radiates terror and shows clear signs of excessive drug use. Uses a cursed bow in battle. Can shoot multiple targets at once and plant seeds of withering arts. When the Corrupted Knight falls, takes up the quest and becomes dramatically stronger. Knights that are hostile to him will shift to Wither.
enemy_1513_dekght_2.ability:
Attacks 2 targets simultaneously
Fires explosive arrows at three targets which explode after a short delay, dealing high Arts damage to the target and surrounding four tiles
Gains greatly increased ATK, ASPD, and Movement Speed when  dies

enemy_1425_lrcmra_2.name: Sacrilegious Chimera
enemy_1425_lrcmra_2.desc: The twisted masterpiece of the Confessarii, this bio-weapon combines multiple pure Sarkaz bloodlines. Originium dust clings to its body, which it spreads along with its endless fury wherever it treads by flapping its great wings.
enemy_1425_lrcmra_2.ability: Continuously causes an area of Originium pollution around itself when affected by Originium pollution or an Active Originium Storm, and attacks deal Arts damage instead.

enemy_1427_lrnazg_2.name: Putrefied Sudaram
enemy_1427_lrnazg_2.desc: The Putrefied Sudaram is the shroud of the fallen. Once bloated from gluttony, it now nourishes its insatiable kin. For the Nachzehrer, consumption and nourishment—greed and devotion—are just two sides of the same dreaded coin.
enemy_1427_lrnazg_2.ability:
Attacks deal Physical damage to all surrounding units.
When inside Originium pollution or an Active Originium Storm, attacks deal Physical damage to all surrounding allied units and all your units inside Originium pollution or Active Originium Storms.

enemy_1425_lrcmra.name: Profane Chimera
enemy_1425_lrcmra.desc: A twisted creation of the Confessarii, this bio-weapon combines multiple pure Sarkaz bloodlines. Originium dust clings to its body, spreading as it flaps its great wings while moving.
enemy_1425_lrcmra.ability: Continuously causes an area of Originium pollution around itself when affected by Originium pollution or an Active Originium Storm, and attacks deal Arts damage instead.

enemy_1424_lrboom.name: Caustic Originium Slug
enemy_1424_lrboom.desc: Mutated Originium slugs around Londinium, altered by the rapidly changing environment. Filled with dangerous Originium particulates, their bodies explode upon death, spreading active Originium pollution.
enemy_1424_lrboom.ability:
Upon death, deals Physical damage in an area and causes Originium pollution.
Increases the strength of an Active Originium Storm when consumed by it.

enemy_1010_demon_2.name: Sarkaz Greatswordsman Leader
enemy_1010_demon_2.desc: A mercenary from Sarkaz, more dangerous than a normal Sarkaz Swordsman. The Originium inside them has boosted their physical abilities, allowing them to wield larger weapons with ease and withstand more damage.

enemy_1071_dftman_2.name: Sarkaz Blademaster
enemy_1071_dftman_2.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary, more skilled than an ordinary Sarkaz Swordsman. Though scarred by years of war, they are still fighting to fulfill the orders of their employers.

enemy_1005_yokai.name: Monster
enemy_1005_yokai.desc: An unmanned enemy drone, called 'Monster' by Reunion forces. Equipped with a long-range Arts receiver, they are likely operated by concealed Casters. Though unlikely to cause heavy damage to armored Operators, they can still pose a considerable threat.

enemy_1005_yokai_3.name: Raptor
enemy_1005_yokai_3.desc: An unmanned enemy drone, its design and name seem to be inspired by a certain space game. enemy_1403_gunsho_2.name: Heavy Rapid Crossbowman

enemy_1184_cadkgt_3.name: The Candle Knight Viviana

enemy_1328_cbjedi.name: Arc Frontliner

Refraction

enemy_1329_cbshld_2.name: Arc Screenguard Captain

enemy_1024_mortar.name: Mortar Gunner

enemy_1006_shield_2.name: Heavy Defender Leader
enemy_1006_shield_2.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers, more dangerous than a normal Heavy Defender. Equipped with a full set of heavy armor, they have ample combat experience and are extremely hard to defeat. As they may have had formal training, proceed with caution.

enemy_1160_hvyslr.name: Dockworker
enemy_1160_hvyslr.desc: Brawny brutes who work at the docks or on Pancho's ships. They enjoy playing cards and drinking, and usually spend the day in a drunken haze. Because they regularly work around seawater, they're more subjected to Erosion.

enemy_1161_tidmag.name: Tidal Caster

enemy_1069_icebrk.name: Yeti Icecleaver

enemy_1058_traink.name: Bully

enemy_1049_eagent_2.name: Agent Leader

enemy_1160_hvyslr_2.name: Dockworker Leader
enemy_1160_hvyslr_2.desc: Giant brutes who work at the docks or on Pancho's ships. They enjoy playing cards and drinking, and usually spend the day in a drunken haze. They're much more robust than regular dockworkers, but they are still quite subjected to Erosion.

enemy_1050_lslime.name: 'Pompeii'
enemy_1050_lslime.desc: A massive infected beast. These aberrations were born in a special high-temperature environment. Nobody wants to approach these creatures due to their grotesque appearance and the extreme heat they give off. The existence of these giant creatures has been known since ancient times, but to see one warped by infection is terrifyingly unforgettable...

enemy_1182_flasrt_3.name: Lazurite, Monique

enemy_1118_lidbox_2.name: Elite Pugilist Prisoner
enemy_1118_lidbox_2.desc: An inmate who wholeheartedly trusts in his fists. Do not underestimate this inmate who is able to utilize both fist and chain in harmony now.

enemy_1306_mhtrtl_2.desc: A Chitotex Clawbeast subspecies particular to certain regions. Their sturdy upper limbs accelerate their excavation of rock exponentially, while their stern shells can shrug off severe batterings. These traits allow them to better expand their territory.

enemy_1509_mousek.name: 'Rat King'

enemy_1060_emouse_2.name: Greytail Leader

enemy_1240_ltgint_2.name: Enhanced Mass-Produced Colossus
enemy_1240_ltgint_2.desc: An Originium Arts creation that sacrifices stability for miniaturization. Very well-equipped for breaching, it is often used to break through enemy lines. Its physical strength has been improved but its weakness to interference remains an issue.

enemy_2026_syudg.desc: The last surviving Saint of Iberia, living well past mortal life expectancy. His longevity came at a price, but he remembers his duty and his enemies, despite having lost his reason.

Based on available data, its weapon module also allows it to block some Arts damage.

enemy_1112_emppnt.name: Imperial Artillery Targeteer
enemy_1112_emppnt.desc: A heavily-armored, sturdily-built drone operated by artillery casters of the Ursus army. They presage the withering firepower of the Ursus Artillery.

enemy_1321_wdarft_2.name: Decaying Collector Disciple
enemy_1321_wdarft_2.desc: An organic construct containing the Nachzehrer King's Originium Arts. It can extract all kinds of dead souls from the battlefield to create Seeds of Decay.

enemy_1070_iced.desc: An unmanned, slow-flying enemy drone equipped with a special warhead released upon approaching Operators that inflicts Arts damage and a freezing effect in an area. enemy_1005_yokai_2.name: Monster Mk II
enemy_1005_yokai_2.desc: An unmanned enemy drone that has been upgraded from its namesake model, the Monster MkII. Though unlikely to cause heavy damage to armored Operators, they can still pose a considerable threat.

relics.name: Featured Canned Meat

relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +1, all allied units gain +1% ATK

relics.desc: Dossoles' famous coffee-flavored candy with actual caffeine. Perfect for getting through that 15 hour shift.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +2, all allied units gain +2 ASPD

relics.name: Screaming Cherry
relics.desc: A Mama John's candy. Though the niche vinegar-pickled cherry flavor can't compete with the best-sellers in popularity, it has garnered a small group of loyal supporters. Rumor has it that one of Mama John's directors loves this flavor.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +2, all allied units gain +2% ATK

relics.name: Pitt's Assorted Fruits
relics.desc: With rich taste and affordable prices, Pitt's assorted fruit candies rival Mama John's in the sweets market. Muelsyse's favorite.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +3, all allied units gain +3% ATK, DEF, and HP

relics.name: Topographical Map
relics.desc: A hand-drawn map exuding antiquity. The labels and terrain are enlarged, and its scale seems to be off.

relics.name: Conch of Divine Whispers
relics.desc: Sound it, and gods you've never seen or even heard of will answer your call. Actually, it's just a hollow conch shell.
relics.usage: Deployment Limit +1, all allied units gain +4% HP

relics.name: Church Alms Meal Voucher
relics.desc: In the declining coastal towns of Iberia, certain churches will provide alms in the form of meals, which their believers can redeem using vouchers. As for how these vouchers are distributed, that is a secret.
relics.usage: Deployment Limit +2, all allied units gain +6% HP

relics.name: Pried Open Toolbox
relics.desc: The tool box of the troupe's prop master. From nails to dummy props, it has everything.
relics.usage: Hope +4

relics.name: Antiquated Sheet Music
relics.desc: A tattered piece of sheet music. The notes can no longer be clearly read. No one knows for certain what story this piece was meant to tell.
relics.usage: Hope +4

relics.name: Letter of Termination Contract
relics.desc: You can break free from this troupe with just one signature.
relics.usage: Hope +4

relics.name: Universal Key
relics.desc: An old, silver key. It can open any door. As the keeper, the troupe's butler naturally needs a key that unlocks all the secrets here.
relics.usage: Hope +6

relics.name: Banshee's Kiss
relics.desc: Outbloods show exceptional courage when in possession of this trinket, but to Logos, a rare male Banshee, it's just a common object.
