endings.name: The End?
endings.desc: The unfinished tale is abruptly halted by the Lord of the Liches. He prefers your focus on building the present rather than appeasing departed souls.
cond: Default ending 「The End?」. On any difficulty, clear the floor 5 Dreadful Foe 「Emergency Lecture」 or 「Cognitive Correction」 while holding none of 「Time and Light」, 「Anasa's Karma」, or 「Endless Key」.
The second-to-last node of floor 5 is always a Prophecy ('The Last Page') — here, do NOT speak the Demon King's name.

endings.name: Twin Crowns
endings.desc: Your bold assumptions and imagination have brought this ending to life. Your soul quivers with excitement, your mind in a trance, leaving you in awe as you slowly comprehend what has transpired.
endings.change: The twin kings bring reconciliation to the land, where all beings live and love in harmony. After two centuries of suffering, an epoch of humanity finally dawns, allowing healing to begin. This is the dream home the Sarkaz are proud to share.
cond: First achieve the ending 「The End?」 to unlock the related choices and collectibles.
「Coronation」: always appears at the floor 2 Prophecy — choose 'The two crown each other' to obtain the relics 「Pure-White Petals」 and 「Wordless Deed」.
At any False to Truth node, decode 「Pure-White Petals」+「Wordless Deed」 once to obtain the collectible 「Pledge of Babel」.

In 'The Last Page', choosing to speak the Demon King's name changes the floor 5 Dreadful Foe to 「Court Visit」 (「The Court of Souls」 with the ancestor relic) and the final bosses to 「Theresis, Black Crowned Lord」 & 「테레시아, 검은 왕관의 성현」.
Achieved by clearing floor 5 while holding none of 「Time and Light」, 「Anasa's Karma」, or 「Endless Key」.

endings.name: City of Angels
endings.desc: Sarkaz and Sankta are one. This strange tale fascinates you so much that even the revenants' wrath and the involvement of the Lord of the Liches can't pull you away...
endings.change: A gun fires, and both the nouns 'Sankta' and 'Sarkaz' are lost, leaving only a radiant and divine word in their wake: Teekaz.
cond: Achieve the ending 「The End?」, then unlock 「현실 치환」 in 「역사 재구축」 (creates Lost and Found nodes during exploration).
At a Lost and Found node, pick the square box to obtain the collectible 「Prophet Horn」.
Refresh the nodes once and, under the effect of 「Prophet Horn」, a Prophecy ('A Truth to Deliver') appears — spend 2 Concepts on 'Fill the flaw with a Concept' to enter the strange chapter.
Obtain the collectible 「Time and Light」 at the strange director's Prophecy ('Knocking on the Seam').
Achieved by clearing floor 6 (boss: 「Holy City」) without holding 「Endless Key」.

endings.name: Wisdom of Kaśjñāna
endings.desc: Qui'lon shares many tales and parables with you. Today, you enlighten one another. Tomorrow, you accompany him into the home of the Anasa, the land of the Dharma—Katsidiya.
endings.change: Crystals may glimmer, and water may have no ripples. Dependent origination, the realization of the Dharma. Katsidiya's gates now swing wide.
cond: After Expansion I, achieve any ending and unlock 「현실 치환」 in 「역사 재구축」.
At a Lost and Found node, pick the single lotus petal to obtain the collectible 「Petal」 (spends all Originium Ingots; EXP and Ingot gains are halved afterwards).

At a Face-Off node, spend 2 Concepts to enter the special 2v2 battle 「가벼운 배로 함께 건너다」 (flying units only), and defeat the hardest formation (two lotus thrones) to obtain the collectible 「Anasa's Karma」 (Leader/Elite HP +50%, floor 5 boss empowered).
Achieved by clearing floor 6' (boss: 「The Dharma Sermon」) without holding 「Endless Key」.

endings.name: Impeccable Day
endings.desc: 'The Lord of Fiends destroyed all.'—This is what the revenants imagine. But with enough time, if Amiya can truly figure out how to save everything, all life trapped in stasis will be granted salvation.
endings.change: Complete or incomplete, all suffering in all stories ends today.
cond: After Expansion II, achieve any ending.

At the encounter 「Omen in the Void」, choose 'Accept it' to obtain the collectible 「Prophetic Image」 — afterwards, defeating the 「Finale Cadence」 that appears in every battle drops the relic 「Despair」 (refinable 「Salvation」→「Stasis」 at False to Truth by spending Concepts to decode).
Feed it into 「All or Nothing」 (an Encounter) to forge the key, obtaining the collectible 「Endless Key」 plus one of the '종결의' series collectibles.
Clear the final floor while holding 「Endless Key」 to enter floor 7 「Fate's Finale」 (a single node), then defeat 'Amiya' in the reactor finale.

encounters.title: Haunting Cries
encounters.desc: Your surroundings have changed many a time, but you always hear residents repeating the same rumor: a vengeful ghost that sobs as it haunts the streets of Kazdel... Eventually you decide you've heard enough, and go out to satisfy your curiosity. Whereupon you discover... it's just a lost Perro.

next.desc: As you listen to his sobs, you suddenly realize something: You know this voice. This Perro seems to have been... following you the whole time?! You want to ask him why, but he keeps cringing away in silence, and it infuriates you.

His story is one of repeatedly trying to muster the courage to change, only to fall back to square one each time. Full of weepy lament and self-pity, it is hardly original. There's nothing you can do to help, so you console him briefly before leaving.

encounters.title: The Uninvited
encounters.desc: This duck intrudes into your story out of nowhere, his quacks nerve-wracking and his webbed footprints ridiculous. How did he even appear, and what does he actually want?

choices.title: I just want to beat him up

next.desc: He seems to be very familiar with the city. Despite your efforts, you can't so much as pluck a feather off as he dodges all your blows, which infuriates you even more.

choices.title: Forget it, I'm used to it now

next.desc: You can't be bothered with this duck, and leave him be. Soon after, he waddles away and vanishes, leaving a tinkling noise that echoes throughout the city nonstop. Could he actually be part of the city? A duck of Kazdel?

encounters.title: The Silent Champ
encounters.desc: This Ursine is only here in the story because of his noisy Duck boss. He rarely responds with words, because he can solve most issues with just his fists.

next.desc: You decide to set things straight with the Ursine: This is Kazdel, your turf. When it comes to violence, you are the one to be reckoned with, not him.

choices.title: Raise your spearhead!
choices.desc: When possessing Talons of Hatred: I've had enough!

next.desc: The spiky spearhead attracts the Duck Lord's attention. He does not negotiate with you, and simply asks the Ursine to 'take it.'

He doesn't react as you leave, standing there with his arms crossed as usual. He watches his boss jump up and down, waiting for him to eventually leap onto his shoulders so they can vanish into the streets.

choices.title: And what if I don't?
choices.desc: Encounter a special and dangerous battle

encounters.title: Quacking Mad
encounters.desc: This time, both the duck and his accomplices are here. They're stepping on gold... stones... books..., no, why does it keep changing? What witchcraft is the duck employing? What does he want?

next.desc: The duck's henchmen gather around as you approach them—wait, did someone escape already?

choices.title: Stab the duck right through
choices.desc: When possessing Talons of Hatred: I've had enough!

next.desc: Duck Lord seems to fear the spearhead in your hand, and calls his men to be on guard, a rare sight. But it's too late—you decide to unleash all your resentment and wrath on him.

Other than being a tad annoying, he hasn't really gotten in your way, so why bother with him at all? He talks grandly about building Kazdel into a business empire while standing on a pile of shifting objects. You watch him for a moment, then smile and leave. What you fail to notice is that the surrounding decorations are changing too. Though their forms are not final, they are all shaping into the Duck Lord's head.

choices.title: Any last words?
choices.desc: Encounter a special and dangerous battle

encounters.title: The Pious Worshiper
encounters.desc: A pious Sankta nun kneels before a holy statue in soft prayer, completely unaware of your arrival.

choices.title: Approach the nun
choices.desc: Some people should not be here

next.desc: You walk up behind her, still unsure of how to deal with this enemy, but a gunshot going off shatters your doubts. The nun dissipates before your eyes, and you find yourself surrounded by Sankta. Alright... so it was a trap.

choices.title: Exact revenge
choices.desc: When possessing Talons of Hatred: Some people should not be here

next.desc: Naturally, you have no mercy for your enemies. You throw your weapon, but it passes through her, smashing the statue. In an instant, Sankta emerge from the shadows, while she has already vanished into thin air.

A Sankta showing up in Kazdel with a horned holy statue—this strange sight compels you to act cautiously. The nun soon finishes praying and walks towards you. You can tell she is a pure Sankta, but what she places in your hands is a clay Sarkaz figurine. Was she praying for peace and reconciliation between the two races? Is that possible?

choices.title: Good day, my prey
choices.desc: Encounter a special and dangerous battle

encounters.title: Employee of Babel
encounters.desc: Together with his kin, a man named Big Bob has transformed this Babel outpost into a rest station for travelers. However, since they all wear identical gear, you're not sure which one is the real Big Bob. As you prepare to depart, a Babel employee brings you some supplies.

choices.title: Let's make a deal
choices.desc: Use 3 Plans and gain all the Collectibles above

choices.title: I think I've seen this one before!
choices.desc: Lamp of Wishesresponds and grants you a Collectible

choices.title: We need this for our story
choices.desc: When possessing Burdenherd Bell: gain Collectible

choices.title: Is this the one I want?
choices.desc: Have Lamp of Wishes: gain a Collectible

encounters.title: Disappear—
encounters.desc: Only you remain.

encounters.title: Disappear...
encounters.desc: The story collapses.

encounters.title: Disappear.
encounters.desc: Thoughts.

encounters.title: Final Page
encounters.desc: In countless stories and reflections, Kazdel has transformed from an empty shell with just a furnace into a prosperous city. Details and histories that defy reality fill it up, nearly completing it. Now, the only thing it lacks—the piece it needs from you—is its ending. You take a deep breath and...

next.desc: You feel a slap on the back of your head and hear the Lord of the Liches' rebuke. As you see Ermengarde backing away, a sense of imminent dread fills you.

choices.title: Your mouth is covered
choices.desc: The revenants declare the Kings' true names

choices.title: The revenants pour out of your mouth
Declare their true names
choices.desc: There are two Kings of Sarkaz...

choices.title: Nothing but silence
choices.desc: The revenants declare the King's Dharma name

choices.title: A lotus petal falls from your mouth
choices.desc: The revenants turned into lotus petals?!

encounters.title: The Dissident's Sound
encounters.desc: An ancient Guardian Gun lies by the Kazdel Soul Furnace. You have a hunch that firing off the gun will bring about a groundbreaking change.

next.desc: You throw the Guardian Gun into the forge and watch the symbol of the Sankta disappear into the flames. Sarkaz stories should not be sullied by their enemies.

encounters.title: Deciphering Evolution
encounters.desc: The Farchaser's Revelation forever altered the evolution of the Teekaz, but it took time for all of them to grow halos and wings. During this period, kin of both black and white left behind written records. You discover a library full of these files that could help with future explorations. You decide to interpret them with the following method.

choices.title: Read another book
variants: Again! My luck can't be that bad!

choices.title: That's enough
choices.desc: I need some time to digest all this...

next.desc: If the result has already been determined, why does the process even matter?
If the result has already been determined, why does the process even matter?

encounters.title: Tower of Thoughts
encounters.desc: 'These are all ideas nobody wants. Pity they just end up in a pile here.' A Zalak stands atop the trash heap, checking a list off in his hands. 'The boss asked me to pack 'em up and take them away, so if you want something, you best be quick.'

next.desc: You have no plans to waste your time on worthless trash, so you turn and leave.
There might be something useful inside this pile of abandoned ideas. Do you want to keep looking?

You not only refuse to put him down, but even grab his cheeks to give it a good squeeze. Naturally, he gets angry and breaks free from your grasp. He hits a red button, dives into the trash, and vanishes without a trace.
You put the Zalak down, and apologize to him. Soon after you leave, a large swarm of drones moves the trash someplace else.

encounters.title: Highest Bidder Wins
encounters.desc: A crowd gathers outside a tent, their expressions mixed—some ecstatic, others crying in remorse. Curious about the commotion, you move closer, but Gopnik stops you. 'The Duck Lord is holding an auction,' he explains, 'and there's only one item left. You need to pay an entrance fee to start bidding.' He adds curtly, 'This event is reserved for the successful.'

next.desc: You have a plan for each and every Ingot in your pocket—and they're definitely not going to be spent here.

choices.title: Pay the Duck Lord and take the auction item
variants: Lose 25 Originium Ingots but gain a Collectible
Lose 15 Originium Ingots but gain a Collectible
Lose 35 Originium Ingots but gain a Collectible

choices.title: Up the bid by 5 Originium Ingots

next.desc: Gopnik doesn't seem to understand you, so you try again, hoping he doesn't get angry. But your explanation falls on deaf ears, and Duck Lord runs out from behind the curtains in a huff, snatching the sign from your hands. Gopnik puts down his gavel and begins cracking his knuckles.

'Just kidding, you say...?' Someone grabs you by the collar, and your feet leave the soft carpeted floor. 'Just kidding?' Duck Lord seats himself with an arm raised up high, and sentences you with a downward gesture. A gang of thugs armed with bats immediately surrounds you. The one with a bruised and bitter face seems to be the spotlight operator.

Squeak—! Duck Lord jumps off his seat and stares at your face. He gestures with his hand, and screams sound from above before the spotlight refocuses on him. However, he does not name a new price, and throws the sign onto the ground before angrily stomping backstage. The gavel falls, and Gopnik walks into the lit circle to announce that you have won the final item behind the curtain for thirty-five ingots.

encounters.title: Truth to be Told
encounters.desc: You notice a rift open up in a corner of Kazdel, an immature and incomplete concept brought to life. You watch the scene unfolding inside the gap and decide to—

choices.title: Fill the gap with your plans

next.desc: The gap expands until it replaces the original landscape. Meanwhile, a vague sense of connection spreads in your mind... Whatever it is, a new story begins.
The gap soon disappears, and what remains in your mind are only fragments of its existence.

encounters.title: Coronation
encounters.desc: 'The previous King of Sarkaz is no more, and the black crown floats between Theresa and Theresis. The power of Civilight Eterna awaits a wielder, the Sarkaz await their King, and Kazdel awaits its master. And so, we witness—'

choices.title: The two of them crowning each other
choices.desc: Gain Lingerers: Pure-White Petals and Wordless Deed

encounters.title: Courtyard of the Departed
encounters.desc: War, disease, and accident have constantly wrested friends away from Theresis and Theresa. To commemorate the fallen, the two Kings have erected a memorial park in the center of Kazdel. Here, you see monuments dedicated to their close friends. You walk before one, and look carefully at the name of the departed—

encounters.title: Omen in the Void
encounters.desc: Nymph strolls the boundary of imagination, hoping for a rare lightbulb moment. The storyteller shares many stories with the revenants, but when she has already narrated truth, adaptation, and imagination, what else is there? The revenants linger around her, constantly examining and modifying the ideas the storyteller is unwilling to bring to light. For example, a prophecy regarding the Sarkaz King...

encounters.title: All or Nothing
encounters.desc: Pulling the story's loose threads while blindly searching for the end—the end is but an incoherent contradiction. By the time Nymph realizes this, the ground beneath her has already turned to layers of chaos. The desire for an end awakens the ending. From within the void, it models on 'Amiya' as it traces the narrator's own end. To stop it, to seal it back within the void, Nymph needs to...

choices.title: Forge a key with the beginning
variants: Lose Despair, obtain Framework of The End and Endless Key
Lose Despair, obtain Framework of The End

choices.title: Stop narrating
choices.desc: Lose Despair, obtain a random Collectible

encounters.title: Ominous Portal
encounters.desc: The outline of a door materializes on the wall, with witchcraft energy seeping from its cracks. Is this a form of reconnaissance, an attempt to traverse through time and space, or the creation of a powerful entity? The important question isn't who they are, but how you will confront them.

choices.title: Open the door
choices.desc: How will I confront what's inside?

next.desc: This creation has restrictions set in place, so both wisdom and violence are required to open it.

choices.title: Destroy its vessel
choices.desc: How should I destroy this illusion?

next.desc: This creation has restrictions set in place, so both wisdom and violence are required to open it.

choices.title: Enter battle
variants: Encounter a special and dangerous battle

choices.title: Stay away
choices.desc: How should I forget that glimpse?

next.desc: Indulging your curiosity can be dangerous, even in your fabricated tale. You decide to avoid this strange phenomenon.

encounters.title: Good Fortune To You
encounters.desc: The resplendent temple enshrines a statue of an Anasa forged from darksteel. According to some Ancients worshiping by the road, the Tripartite Darksteel Heavenly King presides over wealth and commerce. One need only speak a reverent prayer or rejoice in one's heart to receive his blessing, and good fortune shall come to you.

choices.title: Offer some of your belongings
choices.desc: Lose 8 Originium Ingots, but receive 2 random Collectibles

choices.title: Offer all of your belongings
choices.desc: Lose all Originium Ingots, but gain 3 random Collectibles

choices.title: Offer a prayer
choices.desc: Have Doodle of Hope: gain 3 random Collectibles

next.desc: You have no idea who this Anasa is, and you're uninterested in listening to the explanations of the two nearby priests with lazuli hair. After a quick stroll around the temple, you leave.

encounters.title: Desire to Express
encounters.desc: Revenants suddenly appear in your tale. They rampage through it, tearing out a chunk of the sky and throwing it onto the ground. In its place, they construct a giant 'Portal', and countless smaller 'Portals' pour out from it, swallowing up the living and spitting them out in other times. They'll drive you mad with their antics before they stop.

choices.title: Resist as best you can
choices.desc: Use 2 Plans and gain a Collectible 「Rolling Ancestors」

next.desc: After Tin Man talks things over with them, the revenants quickly leave.

encounters.title: Slugkaz!
encounters.desc: 'They say that Originium has shaped the Teekaz over eons, making them what they are today. Unknown to many, Originium has bestowed upon the Teekaz more than one form. In the twisting pipes and obscure corners of the Soul Furnace, amidst piles of century-old Originium waste, dwells a distant cousin of the modern Sarkaz—the arachnid type'

choices.title: A stripped spider leg appears from the darkness!
Did you hear something just now?
How can that be?
choices.desc: There's no way Originium slugs are our cousins!

next.desc: Perhaps an 'Originium Slug epoch' may have existed, but we have long missed it.

choices.title: Did it just tell me to 'grab some lunch'?
Did it just write 'stay safe' on the ground?

encounters.title: Shortcut
encounters.desc: 'The pipes of the Soul Furnace sprawl wide and are infested with all sorts of odd creatures. Perhaps a sentient pipe truly exists, promising travelers safety and comfort at its end. Yet, fate dictates that each step on that path only brings them closer to a gruesome end... What do you think?'

choices.title: It's a lie to lure people in, right?

next.desc: 'This is Kazdel, where all promises inevitably turn true—that's what they told me, anyway. Hmph, take a different path then. Like this one that leads to some ruins, or that one that's a dead end.'

encounters.title: Burdenbeast Traveler
encounters.desc: A burdenbeast loaded with luggage stands atop the city ruins, calmly shaking the snow off its body. Where did it come from? How did it get up there? It obviously cannot talk, but it snorts and a notebook falls off its back. It says 'Hiking Across Terra! Next stop: Kazdel ruins! Looking for guides, compensation negotiable!' The inside pages are filled with stamps, the last one hailing from a certain Snowcap Post Office in Kjerag.

choices.title: Travel with the burdenbeast
choices.desc: Squad Size Limit +1, Fully-loaded burdenbeast joins you

choices.title: Ask for a down payment
choices.desc: Gain 5 Originium Ingots, Fully-loaded burdenbeast joins you

next.desc: You turn to leave but can't resist a glance back: the burdenbeast remains atop the ruins, doing nothing. You're more convinced than ever that this is a scam—since Kazdel descended underground, swindlers have targeted foreign treasure hunters with all kinds of tricks. You just didn't expect them to use such a creative method.

encounters.title: Crater Treasure
encounters.desc: A dirt-covered Sarkaz stops you and asks if you'd like to broaden your horizons. 'I dug up all these from the depths of Kazdel—actual artifacts from the Royal Courts.' He produces a glowing stone, proudly showing the Royal Court's imprint. Seeing your skeptical gaze, he grits his teeth and promises to throw in a ticket to the 'Lost Kazdel Museum.' 'It's a popular attraction, you know? You'll definitely get your money's worth!'

choices.title: Alright, I'll take one
choices.desc: Lose 5 Originium Ingots and gain a Lingerer

choices.title: What do you think, Carrot?
choices.desc: When possessing Burdenherd Bell: gain a Lingerer

choices.title: Isn't this a regular rock? Don't try to fool me

next.desc: 'I would've let you go if you were an explorer, but now...' The dirt-covered Sarkaz waves his hand impatiently and a group of Sarkaz wielding hoes and sickles surrounds you. 'I'm sure they're loaded with ingots. Make sure you squeeze them dry!'

choices.title: I've no interest in your tricks

next.desc: You insist on leaving and the Sarkaz does not stop you. As you pass him by, you notice there's no dirt under his nails despite his dirt-covered body. You realize something and quicken your pace.

encounters.title: Popular Attraction
encounters.desc: You see a strangely shaped arch gate with the words 'Lost City Kazdel' welded onto its rusty frame. You find a shabby ticket booth with no line and a sleepy child inside. He shoots you a glance and says: 'Free King of Sarkaz souvenir with each ticket, only three 'clinkers'. No refunds.' You search your pockets, but find nothing you'd call a 'clinker'.

choices.title: Will this work as a ticket?
choices.desc: Lose Nothing To Say and gain a random Collectible

choices.title: Sneak in from the side
choices.desc: Who says I need a ticket to get in?

next.desc: Past the arches, a winding staircase leads downward, flanked by slipshod murals telling the increasingly absurd story of Kazdel's crash. Deeper in, you find yourself on a viewing platform. By torchlight, you see a 'palace' made of leaves and straw atop a rocky outcrop, complete with a crooked sign proclaiming: 'Actual Site for the King of Sarkaz's Coronation. Crowns sold at the ticket booth.'

choices.title: Leave exasperated
choices.desc: Why don't I have even a single 'clinker'?

next.desc: You walk away, increasingly puzzled: What exactly is a 'clinker'? Why is this the first time you've heard of it? Seeking answers, you return and ask the child, who bursts into laughter: 'Clinkers are clinkers! If you don't have one, what's that on your shoulders?' Your head suddenly feels dizzy, and your neck seems unbearably heavy. Too scared to ask further, you quickly leave.

choices.title: Give the ticket seller a piece of your mind

next.desc: You angrily return to the ticket booth, but now see a Sarkaz covered in dirt inside. He whistles a tune as he picks at his nails, paying your complaints no mind. When you ask for a refund, he suddenly takes out a knife and jabs it into the table: 'Having a laugh, are we? No refunds! Unless you let me gouge out your eyes and unsee my exhibit!'

choices.title: Suppress your anger and leave
choices.desc: Fool me twice, shame on me. I'm never coming back!

next.desc: As you pass by the ticket booth, you notice a dirt-covered Sarkaz inside instead of the child. He looks familiar, but you want nothing more to do with this place, so you pretend he doesn't exist and keep walking. When you're far enough away, you hear a muffled sound behind you. Turning back, you see the arch gate has vanished, leaving only a deep hole in the ground.

encounters.title: Epochal Revision
encounters.desc: 'Look, this is one of the many tomes that the Sarkaz has written to hold their prophecies. Let's see, destroyed homeland, cruel enemies, the usual stuff... Wait, these are unlucky incidents... that all happened to me recently?! Could this book be foretelling my fate? I wonder if I can change my luck if I did some revisions...'

choices.title: 'If Originium was edible...'
choices.desc: Lose 5 Originium Ingots and enter Prosperous Epoch

choices.title: 'If the land united into one...'
choices.desc: Lose 1 Plan and enter Prosperous Epoch

choices.title: 'If, tomorrow, I run into...'
choices.desc: 'But am I willing to be at the mercy of prophecy?'

next.desc: 'Best not add anything! What's so bad about a little misfortune?'

encounters.title: Eve of Defense
encounters.desc: Kazdel's city walls are being reinforced to resist the coalition's invasion. Right after you put a roadblock into place, a Sarkaz comes by. 'Hey, friend. Wanna know about something that just happened in the city?'

choices.title: What is it?
choices.desc: Gain 5 Hope and fight a different Dreadful Foe

encounters.title: Shifting City
encounters.desc: Kazdel's city structure defies logic. Roads stretch based on intuition, and the scenery mirrors one's subconscious. Walking through it feels like reflecting on time and space. Consequently, many lose their way, while the city itself watches countless bewildered travelers with great interest.

next.desc: You deconstruct the city's logic with your plans and uncover its principle: In this perceivable space, one's creativity condenses into tangible bricks that pave the city. These bricks change with each shift in thought, reshaping the city with every change. You apply this understanding to the road ahead, and Kazdel reveals a path to somewhere familiar—

choices.title: Try to win over the city
choices.desc: Lose 1 Plan and gain 10 Originium Ingots

choices.title: Seek help from the Child of Soil and Stone

next.desc: The Child of soil and stone built this city, so naturally she knows how exactly it works. You soon leave the place with her guidance.

encounters.title: Future Hunter
encounters.desc: A Cyclops tears open a rift and appears before you. 'Greetings.' Her manner is polite, but her tone carries an edge. 'You have it, don't you? The key that can change the future.' Though she doesn't look directly at you, her gaze feels piercing. 'Please hand it over so I can alter the bleak future I foresee. Do not refuse me.'

next.desc: 'I'm sorry, but I must have it, even if I have to resort to violence.' The Cyclops waves her staff and vanishes into the fog. The mist dissipates, mapping out a conflict with you within.

choices.title: Present the drawing representing hope
choices.desc: Lose Doodle of Hope and gain 8 Max Life Points

choices.title: Present the spearhead representing hatred
choices.desc: Lose Talons of Hatred and obtain a Promotion Voucher

encounters.title: Story to be Told
encounters.desc: You notice a rift open up in a corner of Kazdel, an immature and incomplete concept brought to life. You watch the scene unfolding inside the gap and decide to—

choices.title: Fill the gap with your plans

next.desc: The gap expands until it replaces the original landscape. A new story begins.

choices.title: Absorb it into your thoughts
choices.desc: Fruitless branches will become sustenance for other stories

next.desc: The gap soon disappears, and what remains in your mind are only fragments of its existence.

encounters.title: Fated Encounter
encounters.desc: The black beast clamps its jaws down on its prey's throat, until it stops struggling and goes limp. Suddenly, it sees a flash of gold, a creature it has never seen before on the wastes. Under the sun, the strange creature's mane shines, its fangs and claws powerful. Curious, the black beast puts down the prey, locks eyes with the creature, and...

choices.title: Shares its prey with the creature
Runs away with its prey in its jaws

next.desc: And so, it would be a few more days later before the fated encounter between the Teekaz and the Elders.

encounters.title: Dealings in Blood
encounters.desc: This is the Scar Market, where the mercenaries operate. The cutthroats are at your disposal, as long as you can afford them.

choices.title: Recruit
choices.desc: Lose 2 Originium Ingots and recruit one Operator (from a selection of 2 random classes)

choices.title: Promise future compensation
choices.desc: Lose 2 Plans and recruit any Operator

choices.title: Deliver the burdenbeast and loot
choices.desc: When possessing the Burdenherd Bell: Recruit any Operator

choices.title: I'm just here to see the sights

next.desc: The Scar Market is built deep underground, with countless chains keeping it fixed above the lava. A rare and marvelous sight for your eyes: no matter where you go, the Scar Market seems as stable as an anchor... You leave once you satiate your curiosity.

encounters.title: Scarmarket Nexus
encounters.desc: The ancient Sarkaz mercenary group has reformed themselves, now appearing on Terra as 'Scarmarket Nexus'. You've obtained their membership, so now you can do some hiring for free.

next.desc: You've only come to tour the place, so the receptionist arranges a guide for you. A mercenary named Franka takes you around the high-speed battleship they use as a base of operations.

choices.title: Say hello to the boss
choices.desc: When possessingDoodle of Hope: gain 2 Hope, recruit any Operator

encounters.title: A Small Skiff
encounters.desc: You consider paying your respects at a nearby temple, but can't find the way there. As you wander back and forth, you stumble upon a small boat among the reeds. The Anasa ferrywoman is willing to take you to the temple for free, except there's only space for one more on her tiny boat.

choices.title: Work out a way
choices.desc: Have Doodle of Hope: promote 2 Operators

encounters.title: Lost Treasure
encounters.desc: In a crowded art salon, an artist introduces his oil paintings based on ancient legends to passing viewers. Which work would you like to hear about?

choices.title: 'Lord of the Sarkaz'
choices.desc: Lose 5 Life Points and gain 2 King's Collectibles

choices.title: 'King of Terra'
choices.desc: Only when current Life Point is 1: gain all King's Collectibles

encounters.title: Decision in Despair
encounters.desc: A battered line of Sarkaz clad in black slowly inches forward. With most of their bloodlines lost, the Sarkaz struggle to find a way out. You seek guidance from the sages for a path to survival. The Banshee takes out a bone pen, the Lich gazes into the void, and the Cyclops opens his lone eye. After a moment of contemplation, you decide to—

next.desc: Following the flute's melody, the procession continues its march, crossing a river valley, entering a cave, and heading deep underground. If the land under the sun will not allow the Sarkaz to grow, then let this domain of darkness become their home.

choices.title: Follow the Lich's wisdom
choices.desc: When possessing Talons of Hatred: Lose 4 Life Points and gain a special Collectible

choices.title: Follow the Cyclops's prophecy
choices.desc: When possessing Doodle of Hope: recover 4 Life Points and gain a special Collectible

next.desc: You fail to get an answer from the sages. Since no one can point to a certain future, the only thing left to do is keep moving on, for at least the path right ahead is still clear. And so the Sarkaz continue to roam the land, their dream of building Kazdel strong but distant.

choices.title: A gift from the collective self

encounters.title: Plausible But False
encounters.desc: You go through a Door and land on a ship's deck. It seems to be Rhodes Island, but the Kazdelian furnace atop it makes you think it might be a Sarkaz migration ship. You then see a duo walking towards you; one being the Theresa you know so well, but the other is the Feline general who attacked Kazdel...? What's going on?

next.desc: Inside the Door does not belong to the story you're telling, and with a glance at that Feline, you begin to worry if this is actually some kind of Sarkaz nomadic prison. Thus, you escape from the two as you search for an exit the Door left for you, finally returning to your story.

encounters.title: Story's End
encounters.desc: A young Lich perks up when he hears you approaching. 'I finally found someone alive!' He eagerly asks you, 'Can you read? Please tell me how this story ends.' You flip to the last page: A Sarkaz hunter has finally caught up to his prey, a Sankta. This is how it ends—

choices.title: Halos appear on both their heads
choices.desc: When possessing Doodle of Hope: gain 4 Originium Ingots and 2 Hope

encounters.title: Falling Objects
encounters.desc: 'Hey—!' Someone shouts from above, but looking up, all you see are heavy gray clouds. 'Unload more weight from Area C! Damn, are you trying to send Kazdel crashing into the ground?' Soon after, a torrent of miscellaneous objects pours down, followed by a slow rain of clothes and scrap paper. You wait until the downpour ceases before approaching for a closer look.

choices.title: Get help to carry them away
choices.desc: When possessing Doodle of Hope: gain 3 Hope

encounters.title: Kind Lady
encounters.desc: 'We meet again.' A young lady calls out to you. She looks familiar, but you cannot remember her name. 'Don't bother trying to recall. I'm not in your story, so you'll soon forget it even if you do.' She takes out a page, seemingly freshly torn from a book. 'So, will you need my help this time?'

choices.title: Please help me and my companions
choices.desc: When possessing Doodle of Hope: gain 4 Max Life Points

choices.title: I don't need help
choices.desc: I'm good for now, maybe next time.

next.desc: 'Alright, I guess I came for nothing again.' The young lady shrugs. 'I hope you can go further this time; everyone's waiting for the rest of the story.' She stamps her foot and vanishes. At that very moment, you seem to recall her name, but forget it right away.

encounters.title: After the Story Ends
encounters.desc: The Wendigo warrior fought till his last breath, and died with his hopes and dreams. Only a husk remains, and his story has come to an end. But you get to witness what happens after the story ends. You see—

choices.title: The warrior's body disintegrates into ash
The warrior's horns take root and grow
The epoch frozen for eternity
choices.desc: This is not where I want to go

next.desc: No fowlbeast flies, and no wind stirs. You wait for a long time, but nothing changes. No changes will occur—not when you leave, not when the land crumbles, not even when the sun is no more.

encounters.title: Inauguration Ceremony
encounters.desc: Night descends swiftly, and a hot wind stirs ashes and torn page fragments. Tonight marks the first night since Kazdel ascended into the sky. The bare soil bears no trace of the city, only hundreds of glowing iron barrels scattered across the landscape. You see Sarkaz engineers throwing stacks of blueprints into the flames. Some of the engineers cast wary glances behind you. Turning around, you face a group of ominous outlanders. An engineer on watch demands to know why you're here, and you respond—

choices.title: Hand over the floating city blueprints!
We're here for the seminar
choices.desc: When possessing Doodle of Hope: gain 5 Plans

encounters.title: Turning Point
encounters.desc: Cowards inevitably perish in a hateful age. Blood kin slaughtered one after the other, along with anyone keeping their killers away. The enemy did not take his life, if only to derive joy from this twisted joke. All he can do is clutch a photo of his family tightly in his arms. Or is it?

choices.title: Put the photo down and stand up
choices.desc: Gain +5 Toil Limit, and obtain 2 Deathmatch

choices.title: Look at the photo again
choices.desc: Have Doodle of Hope: gain +5 Toil Limit, obtain 2 Deathmatch and Bane of Hatred

next.desc: He is but an ordinary man, bereft of the courage to resist the embodiments of violence. Not long after, he ushers in his own tragic end. But in an era where the culture is violence, none will mourn him.

encounters.title: Grace Before Fire
encounters.desc: Whether around a campfire, a bright lamp, or an energy-transporting furnace node, the Sarkaz can always find a place to gather, talk, and trade, even in the gravest times. You and your group find an empty spot among the crowd and sit down, resting briefly and contemplating your next steps.

choices.title: Get plenty of rest
choices.desc: Max Life Points + 3, end current Epoch

encounters.title: The Origins
encounters.desc: The hunter is now the hunted. Shambling through the silver mounts, each step brings him closer to death. But amidst the relentless metallic onslaught, something seems destined to lead him out of this lethal peril, and that something is—

choices.title: A trinket
choices.desc: What will you abandon for your story?

choices.title: That isn't a trinket, but an organism
A lotus petal
choices.desc: What are you willing to pursue for your story?

next.desc: The petal floating on the water points east, hinting at the solution to the Teekaz's troubles. Yet, the hunter sees only the water in the stone bowl. He drinks it all, then continues into the mountains... After the rain, the bowl refills, and the petal floats anew, awaiting the next traveler.

choices.title: Teekaz or Anasa is but a thought apart
choices.desc: Lose all Originium Ingots, but gain a collectible. 「Petal」

choices.title: A square box
choices.desc: What will you create for your story?

next.desc: Crimson patterns emerge on the black box while white light appears on his head and back. As the ebony hunter finds enlightenment, order is established, and a paradise begins to form. He takes the square box back to his tribe to enlighten his kin and share the glory with Empathy. In the end, the Holy City of Kazdel stands proud, with the hunter's sacred horn resting where he found enlightenment.

choices.title: A pile of metal
choices.desc: What will you lose for your story?

next.desc: The broken pieces of metal can no longer shoot deadly flames, allowing the hunter to venture deeper into the mountains unharmed. In the days to come, he will be crowned king.

choices.title: That isn't a trinket, but a spearhead
That isn't a trinket, but a drawing
Is that a horn, or a gun?

encounters.title: Soul Tales: Eye For an Eye
encounters.desc: The Tin Man has gathered some scattered memories and reconstructed them into a story. Would you like to experience them?

next.desc: A Vampire once imprisoned by the Crimson Troupe now plots her revenge. She will drench them in their own blood, an end befitting their name.
Kazdel's future is too enticing for you to be distracted by other tales just yet.

choices.title: Toss the lotus petal into the tale
choices.desc: Lose 2 Plans. Which way will it head now?

encounters.title: Soul Tales: Unity of Extremes
encounters.desc: The Tin Man has gathered some scattered memories and reconstructed them into a story. Would you like to experience them?

next.desc: Two Sarkaz from the east journey west in search of truth. One seeks enlightenment under a tree, while the other indulges in secular pleasures. Despite frequent clashes, they trust each other with full confidence to watch their backs in battle.
Kazdel's future is too enticing for you to be distracted by other tales just yet.

choices.title: Toss the lotus petal into the tale
choices.desc: Lose 2 Plans. Which way will it head now?

encounters.title: Soul Experiences: Twin Gargoyles
encounters.desc: The Tin Man has gathered some scattered memories and reconstructed them into a story. Would you like to experience them?

next.desc: This pair of Gargoyle cousins are renowned Catastrophe Messengers in Kazdel. Arturia excels in stone-shaping Arts, while Federico is known for his rock-solid perseverance. Their conflicting personalities often lead to arguments, but their disagreements are matched only by their unwavering solidarity against Catastrophes.
Kazdel's future is too enticing for you to be distracted by other tales just yet.

choices.title: Toss the lotus petal into the tale
choices.desc: Lose 2 Plans. Which way will it head now?

encounters.title: Present and Future
encounters.desc: The story's content is ever-changing, its uncertainty is both a boon and a curse. As such, while we immerse ourselves in the present, someone has to go into the strange future and scout the way.

encounters.title: Warm and Cozy
encounters.desc: One anecdote ends, and a new story has yet to begin. For now, what you see is just the warm flames of the furnace from your memories.

items.name: Pure-White Petals
items.usage: Interpret together with Wordless Deed to obtain Pledge of Babel
items.desc: 'A new world.'

items.name: Wordless Deed
items.usage: Interpret together with Pure-White Petals to obtain Pledge of Babel
items.desc: 'Your flowerbed.'

items.name: Daybreak Sun
items.usage: Rarity +1 when you carry this Lingerer onto a new floor. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'If you always leave early and return late, you'll never have time to enjoy the sun.'

items.name: Wild Grass by the Door
items.usage: Rarity randomly shuffles when you carry this Lingerer onto a new floor. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'They say I'm like grass. I want to see what grass looks like.'

items.name: Memorial Stones
items.usage: Rarity +2 when you carry this Lingerer onto a new floor. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'I should have gone up that tower, but I fell before its feet.'

items.name: Nemesis Blood
items.usage: Toil -1 when you carry this Lingerer onto a new floor. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'It's not because I hate him, but because he ought to die.'

items.name: Home's Dust
items.usage: Returns to you on the next floor if lost. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'What did these two words originally mean? Which one of them means 'home'?'

items.name: Shrivel and Sprout
items.usage: Obtains an additional copy of this Lingerer when brought to a new floor. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'You're telling me only now that I have a child?'

items.name: Lead Tag
items.usage: Originium Ingots +3 upon losing this Lingerer. Triggers a special effect in Safe House. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'Knock on the door for me. If someone's willing to open, tell them I'm gone.'

items.name: Cheerful Banter
items.usage: A simple wish. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'Cheer up, I'm about to write off the sum you owe me.'

items.name: Pots In The Ground
items.usage: A simple wish. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'How will they eat now that I'm like this... sigh.'

items.name: Walled Gold
items.usage: A simple wish. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'I just hope they're smart enough to not exchange my gold for those paper scraps.'

items.name: Stored Instruments
items.usage: A simple wish. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'Forget it, I'm sure they won't mess up with our ancestors watching over us.'

items.name: Empty Thoughts
items.usage: A simple wish. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: Less common.

items.name: Nonsensical Ravings
items.usage: A simple wish. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: More common.

items.name: Speechless
items.usage: A simple wish. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'Sure. Whatever.'

items.name: Sprouting Stem
items.usage: A simple wish. More likely to obtain a Collectible when interpreted
items.desc: 'Spear's heavy, can't lift it. Take it away already, but don't forget to take off the tip.'

items.name: Knotted Rope
items.usage: When you carry this Lingerer, Epochs will occur more often
items.desc: 'When I pull the knot, a year will have passed. Shame I won't be there to see it.'

items.name: Elder's Crown
items.usage: Can be interpreted with Inspirers to become an Inspirer of higher rarity
items.desc: 'I was there your grandfather's whole life, and now it's your turn. Look after your people.'

items.name: Despair
items.usage:
Interpret with a Plan to obtain Salvation
Can be used in Encounter: All or Nothing
items.desc: 'You'll definitely live, p-please believe in this... sob...'

items.name: Salvation
items.usage:
Interpret with a Plan to obtain Stasis
Can be used in Encounter: All or Nothing
items.desc: 'Sleep here for a bit. When we find a cure for Oripathy, you'll be able to wake up healthy.'

items.name: Stasis
items.usage: Can be used in Encounter: All or Nothing
items.desc: 'When all of you wake up, there will be no more suffering. Until that day arrives, I will bear the pain alone.'

items.name: Wall
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units gain 1 Shield when deployed
items.desc: 'Remember how to stack these rocks. When they're busy tearing them down, you can run for it.'

items.name: Flames
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have +35 ASPD
items.desc: 'Ha, that won't happen. We have nothing to start a fire with.'

items.name: Relocation
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, ranged Operators gain Camouflage
items.desc: 'Once the Royal Courts finish casting their Arts, just run the same old way. Outsiders won't be able to find us.'

items.name: Oathbreak
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, melee Operators gain ASPD +50 for 5 seconds after taking damage
items.desc: 'I already told them that I'd open the gates. Come in, and kill us all.'

items.name: March
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have +2 push/pull strength
items.desc: 'Come on, walk faster. It's a long journey, and death is waiting.'

items.name: Lost
items.usage: When used: After the next battle, gain 1 additional Inspirer
items.desc: 'If the old won't go, the new won't come! We've been wanting to build a new one anyway.'

items.name: Excavate
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have +1 push/pull strength
items.desc: 'Everyone's gone. Quick, come with me and see if there's anything useful left behind.'

items.name: Witchcraft
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, summon a 'Portal'
items.desc: 'Then, the ancestors entered that silver door, and it's got everything you want.'

items.name: Initiate
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, Deployment Limit +1
items.desc: 'I was a student in Kazdel then, studying comparative literature.'

items.name: Consume
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have +15% Max HP
items.desc: 'We started out farming, and lived on the support layers while the sun was reserved for crops.'

items.name: Reclaim
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have +30% Max HP
items.desc: 'Later, Her Highness fixed up hydroponic equipment, and then we competed with crops for land.'

items.name: Pillage
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have +10% ATK
items.desc: 'What else can we do? Rob them. The Feline, the Caprinae. They rob us back too.'

items.name: Invasion
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have +20% ATK
items.desc: 'The Sarkaz will 'conquer'. What's that? Go to their land, take their things, and stay.'

items.name: Rise and Fall
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units gain +30% ATK and +50% Max HP
items.desc: 'I'm planning to leave the city to make a living.'

items.name: Words
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, gain +25% Command EXP
items.desc: 'Register here. Where are you from? Can you speak Sarkaz? Can you write?'

items.name: Assembly
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, gain +50% Command EXP
items.desc: 'We won't be making deliveries to the frontline? Then... will you still look after us?'

items.name: Catastrophe
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, gain +50% Originium Ingot drops
items.desc: 'What will come is inevitable. The higher ups think it's pointless to drive the city away.'

items.name: Furnace
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, gain +100% Originium Ingot drops
items.desc: 'Basically, the best part of staying in the city is that you won't get cold.'

items.name: Outblood
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have +15 ASPD
items.desc: 'You don't have to remember who has what ears, they'll always recognize you as a Sarkaz.'

items.name: City Driving
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have Redeployment Time -20%
items.desc: 'Remember: never go faster than 5 klicks per hour, or it'll take another decade to fix.'

items.name: Mercenary
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have -2 DP Cost
items.desc: 'Some fight to feed themselves, and some fight to feed their families.'

items.name: Civil War
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units have -3 DP Cost
items.desc: 'Some say it's finally over, but others say it will never end.'

items.name: Respite
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units recover 2% of their Max HP every second after deployment for 20s
items.desc: 'Take a break while you can. When the cannons start firing, you won't have time for one.'

items.name: Slumber
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied units recover 5% of their Max HP every second after deployment for 20s
items.desc: 'I've been worked to the bone my whole life, but now I can finally get a good night's sleep. You guys go ahead and chat; it won't wake me up.'

items.name: Demolition
items.usage: When used: In the next battle,  have 50% less Max HP
items.desc: 'If it's too big to carry, blow it up. The bits will still fetch a decent price.'

items.name: Deathmatch
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, 3 random Operators gain ASPD +100, DEF +50%, RES +25
items.desc: 'Kill you, and I get to live another day. What's so hard to understand?'

items.name: Machine
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, summon 1 Lost Hovercraft
items.desc: 'Press the button, cobble together whatever garbage shoots out, and there you go, you're an inventor.'

items.name: Industry
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, summon 3 Lost Hovercrafts
items.desc: 'These smuggled machines can make kitchenware, musical instruments... With a bit of tinkering, they can all be used in battle.'

items.name: Grand Wish
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, summon a 'Window'
items.desc: 'The ancestors walk into the portal and stand before the window. The land outside is blank, waiting to be filled by their imaginations.'

items.name: Envenom
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, all allied Operators deal an additional 5% of damage dealt as Necrosis
items.desc: 'Remember what you laced your weapons with—don't want to screw yourself over later.'

items.name: Torment
items.usage: When used: In the next battle, enemies under the effects of Stun, Frozen, Frighten, or Fear take 200 Necrosis every second
items.desc: 'That's how those overseers are. The more you yell, the harder they work you.'

items.name: Plans
items.usage: Can be used in Impression Restoration to alter a node or carve a new path
items.desc: 'Remember it well. You'll have to use it one day.'

items.name: Catastrophes
items.usage:
〔Formative〕 More Spines of Epoch will appear; Spines of Epoch and Spikes of Weeping gain +100% Max HP
〔Expansional〕 More Spines of Epoch will appear; Spines of Epoch and Spikes of Weeping gain +150% Max HP
〔Flourishing〕 More Spines of Epoch will appear; Spines of Epoch and Spikes of Weeping gain +200% Max HP
items.desc: In this Epoch, boulders ravage the land and flowers of crystal bloom

items.name: King of Sarkaz
items.usage:
〔Formative〕 Sarkaz enemies gain +20% ATK, Operators on Mark of Epoch cannot actively retreat
〔Expansional〕 Sarkaz enemies gain +35% ATK; Operators on Mark of Epoch cannot actively retreat
〔Flourishing〕 Sarkaz enemies gain +50% ATK; Operators on Mark of Epoch cannot actively retreat
items.desc: In this Epoch, the black crown never vanishes

items.name: Sufferings
items.usage:
〔Formative〕 All allied units lose 25% of current HP on deployment (Effect only triggers once before the unit leaves the field)
〔Expansional〕 All allied units lose 50% of current HP on deployment (Effect only triggers once before the unit leaves the field)
〔Flourishing〕 All allied units lose 70% of current HP on deployment (Effect only triggers once before the unit leaves the field)
items.desc: In this Epoch, Oripathy is the least imaginative suffering

items.name: Commerce
items.usage:
〔Formative〕 Rogue Trader will sell items at a higher price; all allied units +2 DP Cost
〔Expansional〕 Rogue Trader will sell items at a higher price; all allied units +3 DP Cost
〔Flourishing〕 Rogue Trader will sell items at a much higher price; all allied units +5 DP Cost
items.desc: In this Epoch, number equals wealth

items.name: Oddities
items.usage:
〔Formative〕 Plans gain +1 toil; all enemies gain +20% HP
〔Expansional〕 Plans gain +1 toil; all enemies gain +30% HP
〔Flourishing〕 Plans gain +2 toil; all enemies gain +50% HP
items.desc: In this Epoch, countless peculiar architectures are built and passed down

items.name: Congestion
items.usage:
〔Formative〕 Deployment Limit -1
〔Expansional〕 Deployment Limit -2
〔Flourishing〕 Deployment Limit -3
items.desc: In this Epoch, the population swells, and space is scarce

items.name: Philosophy
items.usage:
〔Formative〕 You may fail to interpret thoughts in False to Truth nodes. The chance of encountering Overflowing Distraction is slightly higher
〔Expansional〕 You may fail to interpret thoughts in False to Truth nodes. The chance of encountering Overflowing Distraction is somewhat higher
〔Flourishing〕 You may fail to interpret thoughts in False to Truth nodes. The chance of encountering Overflowing Distraction is higher
items.desc: In this Epoch, ideas ignite as different schools debate and persuade

items.name: Prosperity
items.usage:
〔Formative〕 Treasure Chests are more likely to appear; allied units gain +20% Max HP
〔Expansional〕 Treasure Chests are more likely to appear, allied units gain +30% Max HP
〔Flourishing〕 Treasure Chests are more likely to appear, allied units gain +50% Max HP
items.desc: In this Epoch, all needs are met and life itself prospers

items.name: Paradox
items.usage:
〔Formative〕 Allied units are harder to discern
〔Expansional〕 Allied units are harder to discern
〔Flourishing〕 Allied units are harder to discern
items.desc: In a story that lacks coherence, the illogical makes perfect sense
